mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 8.0 percent, labor market deterioration triggers immediate equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of observations where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signal collapsing aggregate demand, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with an out-of-sample accuracy exceeding 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase confirms supply-side stabilization, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 60-day forward returns in post-shock recovery periods, with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP crossing below negative two percent confirms severe economic contraction, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth crosses below -2.0 percent
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 45 percent of quarters where GDPC1 declines exceed two percent.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates aggressive real rate compression, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL yields a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly declines in nonfarm payrolls during a financial crisis bottoming phase trigger panic selling, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during crisis bottoming phases, with a hit rate below 48 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output, which directly supports corporate earnings and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows or forward returns show no positive correlation.
